content: @Alice Ryhl I just started looking at the upstreamed linked list work, I've little idea how to use it properly yet, but I was wonder if there is anything stopping one struct having being in two different lists with different attachment points?
also there might be some value in having an example using the linked list that is simpler than binder
ah I guess the ID field is for this
context list looks a bit simpler than the one I was staring at in the rust_binder code

Dave Airlie said:

also there might be some value in having an example using the linked list that is simpler than binder

Yeah, I agree -- I opened a "good first issue" https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux/issues/1121 for this a while ago.
Given the complexity of linked list usage this might be a medium difficulty rather than easy :)

@Alice Ryhl if I wanted to add the ability to insert something after an entry where all I have is the ListArc<T> do you have a recommendation on how that might look? I've played around with a few things, but this is definitely rust at a level slightly beyond me :-)
We don't have an API for that. Note that it would have to be unsafe, since there's no way to check whether the ListArc really is in the list you're looking at.
actually just noticed, is it me or are push_front and push_back both the same, shouldn't they be different (I'll also accept I've been staring at the screen for too long today)
I think the only difference is whether it adjusts self.first.
ah I missed the one line afterwards, makes sense
